name: Sklearn Bayesian Ridge Regression
description: Bayesian Ridge Regression implements a probabilistic linear regression model where model coefficients are treated as random variables with prior distributions. This approach provides robust predictions in scenarios with limited data or multicollinearity and produces uncertainty estimates along with predictions.

fields.name: targetCol
fields.title: Target Column
fields.description: Target column containing the continuous response variable that the Bayesian Ridge Regression model learns to predict.

fields.name: featureCols
fields.title: Feature Columns
fields.description: List of input feature columns used to train the regression model. All features must be numeric, boolean, or vector-compatible.

fields.name: splitRatio
fields.title: Split Ratio
fields.description: Ratio used to split the dataset into training and testing subsets. A value of 1.0 uses the full dataset for training.

fields.name: niter
fields.title: Number of Iterations
fields.description: Maximum number of optimization iterations used to estimate the posterior distribution of model coefficients.

fields.name: alpha1
fields.title: Alpha1
fields.description: Shape parameter of the Gamma prior distribution placed over the precision of the noise (alpha). Controls prior belief about noise variance.

fields.name: alpha2
fields.title: Alpha2
fields.description: Rate parameter of the Gamma prior distribution over the noise precision (alpha). Works with alpha1 to regularize noise estimation.

fields.name: lambda1
fields.title: Lambda1
fields.description: Shape parameter of the Gamma prior distribution over the model weights precision (lambda), influencing coefficient shrinkage.

fields.name: lambda2
fields.title: Lambda2
fields.description: Rate parameter of the Gamma prior distribution over the model weights precision (lambda), balancing model complexity.

fields.name: tol
fields.title: Tolerance
fields.description: Convergence threshold. Training stops when the change in model weights falls below this value.

fields.name: fitintercept
fields.title: Fit Intercept
fields.description: Determines whether an intercept term is included in the regression model. The intercept is not treated as a probabilistic parameter.

fields.name: normalize
fields.title: Normalize
fields.description: If enabled and fit_intercept is true, input features are normalized before regression to improve numerical stability.

fields.name: compute_score
fields.title: Compute Score
fields.description: If enabled, computes the log marginal likelihood at each iteration, useful for convergence diagnostics.
